description_long: This house features a complex, multi-gabled roof with dark gray shingles and light textured exterior walls, giving it a robust and modern suburban appearance. The structure is composed of several interconnected rectangular and L-shaped volumes, creating a dynamic and layered silhouette. The rooflines intersect at various angles, providing architectural interest and depth. Large window openings are visible on some sides, suggesting ample natural light inside, while the main entryway is recessed under a covered porch supported by columns. The exterior finish appears to be a combination of stucco and stone, contributing to a sturdy and weather-resistant facade. The overall design is practical for a residential neighborhood, with a focus on both functionality and curb appeal.
description: A modern, multi-gabled house with dark gray shingle roofing and light textured walls, featuring a layered, intersecting roofline and a covered entry porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gabled roof, layered rooflines, and a recessed entryway under a covered porch.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the broad, flat expanse of the main roof and visible window openings beneath the overhang.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the intersecting gables and the solid, textured back wall with minimal window openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the extended roof sections and the arrangement of smaller gabled volumes.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main entry with a covered porch supported by columns and a simple overhang.
